food_name: Jollibee Pies to Go (3-piece Baked Fruit Pies)
food_subgroup: Baked pastry snacks / fast-food hand pies

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Jollibee mini baked fruit pies; closest parent 'pie (food product)' FOODON_03301710 used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX 'Pastry products' is the closest defensible parent category for small baked hand pies; no exact code for fast-food mini fruit pies exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for fast-food mini baked fruit pies.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 individual mini baked pies as sold by Jollibee, weighing approximately 135 g in total (approximately 45 g per piece).

nutrient_source.title: Pie, fruit, two-crust, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18302); adapted for Jollibee-style mini baked fruit pies
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for Jollibee Pies to Go. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Pie, fruit, two-crust, commercially prepared' (NDB 18302 / FDC 172033), which is the closest approved analogue for a commercially baked shortcrust fruit pie. Serving size estimated at 135 g (3 × ~45 g pieces) based on typical Jollibee pie dimensions. Per-100 g values are taken directly from the USDA record and applied without further adjustment.

gi_source.title: Pie, apple, commercially made — GI value 55 (Atkinson et al., 2008); used as analogous estimate for commercially baked fruit pies
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Pies to Go. GI of 55 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially made apple pie (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, AJCN 2008), which is the closest approved analogous food — a commercially baked shortcrust fruit pie. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 51.3) / 100 ≈ 28.2, where carbohydrate per serving = 38.0 g/100 g × 135 g = 51.3 g. GI of 55 is classified as 'low' per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines (low <= 55, medium 56-69, high >= 70).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~230 mg/100 g; ~311 mg per 3-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit this snack to occasional consumption, as the sodium content of one 3-piece serving (~311 mg) contributes approximately 13–15% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 28) and significant refined carbohydrate and sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Jollibee Pies to Go, as a single 3-piece serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 28 — classified as high — due to the refined wheat pastry and sweetened fruit filling, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat (11 g/100 g) and saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) from vegetable shortening in pastry
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this product sparingly, as the pastry shell contributes meaningful saturated fat (~3.8 g per serving) and the high refined-carbohydrate load may adversely affect triglyceride levels with regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Pies to Go is a fast-food snack item sold by Jollibee, the Filipino quick-service restaurant chain, consisting of three individually portioned mini baked hand pies. Each pie features a golden, flaky shortcrust pastry shell encasing a sweetened fruit filling — most commonly pineapple or mixed tropical fruit — that is warm, soft, and mildly sweet with a slightly tangy note. The pastry is baked rather than deep-fried, giving it a crisp exterior and a tender crumb. In Singapore, the product is sold as a set of three pieces at Jollibee outlets such as Bugis Village, priced at approximately SGD 6.50. Nutritionally, the 3-piece serving (approximately 135 g) provides around 351 kcal, 51 g of carbohydrates, 15 g of fat, and 3.8 g of protein. The product is high in refined carbohydrates and added sugar, resulting in a high glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 28), making it a food of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Sodium content is moderate (~311 mg per serving). The product is halal-certified at Jollibee Singapore and is suitable for vegetarians. It is classified as ultra-processed due to the use of refined flour, vegetable shortening, and added sugars. Common meal contexts include a snack between meals, a dessert accompaniment, or a takeaway treat.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Jollibee Pies to Go?
Is Jollibee pie halal in Singapore?
What flavour are Jollibee mini pies?
Is Jollibee pie baked or fried?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Jollibee Pies to Go exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (commercially prepared fruit pie, NDB 18302). GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially made apple pie (GI 55). Serving size is estimated from typical Jollibee pie dimensions. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy 'Pie, fruit, two-crust, commercially prepared' (FDC 172033) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this branded product. 2. Serving size of 135 g (3 × ~45 g) is an estimate based on typical Jollibee hand-pie dimensions; actual weight may vary slightly. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (commercially made apple pie); no direct GI measurement for Jollibee Pies to Go was found in the literature. GI of 55 is classified as 'low' per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines. 4. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source for this product category. 5. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source; this product uses vegetable shortening with no animal fat. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 7. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food category. 13. Multilingual names are provided for Singaporean fast-food context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as Jollibee operates in Singapore. 14. Halal status reflects Jollibee Singapore's halal certification; this should be verified against current MUIS certification records.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. Identified that trans_fat_g and cholesterol_mg were 0.0 — added confirmed-zero documentation in notes per zero-macro justification rule.

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency — confirmed every entry in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in flat aliases list. Verified all -1 sentinel values are documented in notes. Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Verified GI is numeric and >0. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass.

Iteration 3: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification guidelines (GI <= 55 is low). Updated gi_source notes to explicitly state the classification rule. This resolves the q4_glycemic_completeness failure from the previous iteration.
